items.title: MERT fine-tuning BLOCKED by objective mismatch - cannot break V8 ceiling
items.detail: CLOSED — diagnostic no-op confirmed. LoRA on layers 20-23 has zero gradient when extracting from layers 5-6. val_loss identical across 3 epochs. Recovery failed (Modal scope bug + numpy arch mismatch). ~$30 diagnostic cost. V8 (0.7318) is production. Next: Phase 0 human eval (701 pairs ready) to determine if proxy metric is already good enough.

items.title: MERT fine-tuning feasibility report + dual-model consultation verdict
items.detail: Dual-model consultation complete (Opus 4.6 + Sonnet 4.5). Both agree: 0.73 is near proxy ceiling, taste fusion architecturally broken, DJ transitions > playlists. Verdict: data quality audit FIRST ($50, 1 week), then multi-task architecture, then MERT fine-tune. Full verdict: files/consult-v8-review-verdict.md. Raw: .playwright-mcp/opus-v8-review.md + sonnet-v8-review.md

items.title: C-16 Phase 2 Complete — V8 is production (NDCG@20=0.7318)
items.detail: 7 model variants tested overnight. All within ±2% of V8. Architecture ceiling at ~0.73. MERT audio features dominate. Taste vectors (ALS vs LightGCN), FAISS negatives, directional modeling, listwise loss all ineffective. Next: MERT fine-tuning (GPU) or ship V8 and build product.

items.title: EXP-R01.5 Per-Stem BT completed — test evaluation stubbed, 100 models untested
items.detail: Medium mode ran 7 hours, trained 100 models (4 stems × 25 layers), 11,352 valid pairs. ALL models show test_acc=0 because test evaluation was never implemented (line 284: stub comment). Cannot determine which stem-layer combos predict preferences. Decision needed: (A) Implement eval script + test existing 100 models (~30 min), (B) Re-run experiment with eval built in (~7 hours), or (C) Abandon per-stem hypothesis. Expected: vocals=zero, other/texture dominates (prior stem-ablation ρ=0.214). Target: beat DeepPref 0.4600 by 3+ pp. Models saved: kyma-engine/work/outputs/exp_r01_5_per_stem/
items.context: Research — EXP-R01.5 experiment complete but inconclusive. Needs eval implementation or decision to move on.

items.title: Medi-Cal Rx enrollment email - likely misrouted since Idam is Anthem
items.body: Apr 16 received 'Welcome to Medi-Cal Rx Beneficiary Portal' email from Prime Therapeutics. Idam's insurance is Anthem Blue Cross PPO (Member ID JPU099M91022), not Medi-Cal. Did MEDvidi submit Rx to wrong payer? Check with MEDvidi whether Anthem BCBS is on file as primary insurance for the Lisdexamfetamine prescription - wrong payer billing would explain multiple pharmacy rejections.
items.resolution_note: Not misrouted. Idam IS enrolled in Medi-Cal (BIC card shown Apr 21: 97918535H16086, issued 03/27/2026). Welcome email Apr 16 was legitimate. Flipped diagnosis: MEDvidi pharmacy rejections likely driven by Medi-Cal Rx PA requirements, not generic pharmacy compliance. Mutiat's '12-day compliance delay' may actually be PA paperwork stuck at MEDvidi. New MEDvidi ticket being prepared with prescriber-refused-to-send framing + explicit Medi-Cal PA callout.

items.title: Call Walgreens #01297 back and get verbatim return reason
items.body: (415) 856-0543. Get pharmacist name. Confirm if Rx was returned and when, whether they'll fill a new one pre-attached with ICD-10 F90 + monitoring plan + callback line, and whether they have stock of generic OR Vyvanse. Script at files/medvidi-call-scripts/01-walgreens-recall.txt.

items.title: Call MEDvidi pharmacy escalation line (504) 414-5095 - demand supervisor
items.body: Reference ticket 56379 closure error. Force specific mechanism (prescriber page, supervisor intervention, pre-attached Rx notes). Ask for Medi-Cal PA tracking number + supervisor name + transmission ETA. Script at files/medvidi-call-scripts/02-medvidi-supervisor.txt.

items.title: Parallel call Costco #144 and Safeway #2606 for stock check
items.body: Costco Pharmacy #144 (415) 437-2620, Safeway #2606 (415) 633-1020. Verify: generic lisdexamfetamine AND/OR brand Vyvanse stock, telehealth C-II acceptance policy, what they need for no-hold fill, ETA. Whichever is cleaner becomes Plan B in MEDvidi message. Script at files/medvidi-call-scripts/03-backup-pharmacy-stock-check.txt.

items.title: Activation-quality calibration blocked: raw battery v2 JSON cleared from /tmp
items.body: The raw sources_cited/activation-rank pairs required for Spearman correlation were in /tmp/brain_ab_results/battery-v2-aug-20260417-054618.json, which cleared. Re-running battery v2 (files/consultations/2026-04-17-brain-memory-followup/battery_v2_augmented.py or unified_battery.py) costs ~15 claude-opus-4-5 calls = $5-10, which crosses the autonomous-spend gate (>$5). Need Idam's explicit go-ahead to re-run, OR port the battery outputs that still exist in battery_v2_blind_grading.md into a simpler coarse-grained metric (qualitative PASS/FAIL vs citation count) that doesn't need the raw JSON. Blind-grading file is structured 5 queries x 3 conditions = 15 datapoints but those are quality assessments, not raw sources_cited counts.
